title: 10 Herbal Creams For Altitude Sickness
intro_desc: Herbal creams for altitude sickness are gaining popularity as a natural remedy to alleviate symptoms. These creams often contain herbs like Ginkgo biloba, Zingiber officinale, and Echinacea purpurea, which have been traditionally used to relieve altitude sickness. Ginkgo biloba is known for its ability to improve blood flow to the brain, helping to reduce headaches and dizziness associated with altitude sickness. Zingiber officinale, or ginger, has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to soothe nausea and stomach discomfort. Echinacea purpurea, on the other hand, is believed to boost the immune system, helping the body to adapt to the higher altitude.  When it comes to herbal teas for altitude sickness, they work by stimulating circulation, improving oxygenation of the body, and boosting the immune system. For example, Ginkgo biloba tea can help to increase blood flow to the brain, while Zingiber officinale tea can help to reduce inflammation and alleviate nausea. Echinacea purpurea tea can help to support the immune system, reducing the severity of altitude sickness symptoms. Drinking these teas can provide relief from the symptoms of altitude sickness, allowing you to breathe easier, recover faster, and enjoy your time at high elevations.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba creams contains flavonoids and terpenoids, which are active constituents that help with altitude sickness. These compounds improve blood flow to the brain and reduce inflammation in the blood vessels, making it easier for the body to adapt to high altitudes. The flavonoids also have antioxidant properties, which protect the brain from damage caused by high levels of oxygen and carbon dioxide in the blood. Terpenoids help to thin the blood, reducing the risk of blood clots that can occur at high altitudes. By improving blood flow and reducing inflammation and blood clotting, Ginkgo biloba creams can help alleviate symptoms of altitude sickness.
remedies.image_alt: Ginkgo biloba herbal creams for altitude sickness
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of distilled water, 2 tablespoons of Ginkgo biloba extract, 2 tablespoons of coconut oil, 2 tablespoons of beeswax, and 1 teaspoon of vitamin E oil.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or in a microwave-safe bowl in 10-second increments, stirring between each interval until smooth.
Add the Ginkgo biloba extract and vitamin E oil to the melted mixture and stir well.
Slowly add the distilled water to the mixture, stirring constantly until well combined.
Pour the mixture into a clean, sterilized container and let it cool and solidify before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale creams contains the active constituents gingerol, shogaol, and zingerone, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These compounds help to reduce inflammation in the body, which is a common cause of altitude sickness. Gingerol also has a natural vasodilating effect, which widens blood vessels and improves blood flow to the brain, reducing the risk of altitude sickness. Additionally, zingerone helps to reduce oxidative stress, which can contribute to altitude sickness. By reducing inflammation and improving blood flow, Zingiber officinale creams may provide relief from the symptoms of altitude sickness, such as headaches and fatigue.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal creams for altitude sickness
remedy_procedure: Gather 2 cups of distilled water, 2 tablespoons of grated fresh ginger, 2 tablespoons of coconut oil, 1 tablespoon of beeswax, and 1 teaspoon of vitamin E oil.
Combine the grated ginger and coconut oil in a small saucepan and heat over low heat, stirring until the oil is warm.
In a separate saucepan, melt the beeswax and vitamin E oil over low heat, stirring until the beeswax is fully melted.
Combine the ginger-coconut oil mixture and the melted beeswax mixture, then add the distilled water and stir until well combined.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool and solidify before using the Zingiber officinale cream for altitude sickness.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea creams contains active constituents like alkylamides, caffeic acid, and polyphenols. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help reduce swelling and ease breathing at high altitudes. The polyphenols in Echinacea purpurea creams also have vasodilatory effects, which can improve blood flow to the lungs and increase oxygen delivery to the body. The alkylamides have been shown to stimulate the production of white blood cells, which can help the body fight off infections that may arise due to altitude sickness. By applying Echinacea purpurea creams to the skin, these active constituents can be absorbed and help alleviate symptoms associated with high-altitude conditions.
remedies.image_alt: Echinacea purpurea herbal creams for altitude sickness
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Echinacea purpurea flowers, 1/2 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of shea butter, and 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil.
Melt coconut oil, beeswax, and shea butter in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Remove the melted mixture from heat and add dried Echinacea purpurea flowers. Let it steep for 2 hours or overnight.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a heat-proof bowl. Discard the solids.
Add vitamin E oil to the strained mixture and stir well. Pour the mixture into small containers and let it cool and solidify before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Astragalus membranaceus creams contains a compound called astragaloside IV, which is a key bioactive constituent that helps alleviate symptoms of altitude sickness. Astragaloside IV has anti-inflammatory properties, which can reduce swelling in the brain caused by high altitudes. Astragalus membranaceus also contains isoflavones, such as formononetin and genistein, which can improve blood flow and reduce oxidative stress at high elevations. The saponins in Astragalus membranaceus have been shown to increase the production of nitric oxide, a molecule that helps dilate blood vessels and improve oxygen delivery to tissues. By addressing these underlying mechanisms, Astragalus membranaceus creams may provide relief from altitude sickness symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Astragalus membranaceus herbal creams for altitude sickness
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 2 cups of distilled water, 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of Astragalus membranaceus dried root powder, 2 tablespoons of shea butter, and 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil.
Melt coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Add shea butter and stir until melted. Remove from heat.
Add Astragalus membranaceus dried root powder, vitamin E oil, and distilled water to the melted mixture. Stir well.
Pour the mixture into small containers and let it cool and solidify. Store the Astragalus membranaceus cream in a cool, dry place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra creams contains compounds like glycyrrhizin and 18α-glycyrrhetinic acid. These saponins have anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling in the lungs caused by altitude sickness. Glycyrrhizin also has a diuretic effect, helping the body to eliminate excess fluids that can accumulate in the lungs due to high altitude. Additionally, glycyrrhizin's antioxidant properties can help protect the body from oxidative stress caused by altitude sickness. By reducing inflammation and excess fluids, and protecting the body from oxidative stress, Glycyrrhiza glabra creams may help alleviate symptoms of altitude sickness.
remedies.image_alt: Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal creams for altitude sickness
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of distilled water, 1/4 cup of glycerin, 1/4 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of Glycyrrhiza glabra root extract, 1 tablespoon of vitamin E oil.
Melt beeswax in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl in 10-second increments, stirring between each interval until melted.
In a separate bowl, mix distilled water and glycerin. Add Glycyrrhiza glabra root extract and vitamin E oil. Stir well.
Slowly pour the liquid mixture into the melted beeswax, stirring constantly until combined.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool and harden. Once hardened, use as a cream for altitude sickness.

remedies.remedy_desc: Angelica sinensis creams contains bioactive constituents like ferulic acid, hesperidin, and ligustilide. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help alleviate symptoms of altitude sickness, such as headaches and fatigue. The ferulic acid in Angelica sinensis creams has been shown to improve blood flow and reduce oxidative stress, which can help the body adapt to high altitudes. The hesperidin and ligustilide in the cream have anti-inflammatory effects that can help reduce swelling and pain in the body. By reducing inflammation and improving blood flow, Angelica sinensis creams can help alleviate the symptoms of altitude sickness and promote a faster recovery.
remedies.image_alt: Angelica sinensis herbal creams for altitude sickness
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of Angelica sinensis dried roots, 1/4 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of shea butter, and 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil.
Melt coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl in 10-second increments, stirring between each interval, until smooth.
Add dried Angelica sinensis roots to the melted mixture and let it steep for 30 minutes to 1 hour. Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean bowl.
Add shea butter and vitamin E oil to the strained mixture and stir until combined. Pour the mixture into a silicone mold or a glass jar.
Let the mixture cool and harden completely. Once set, remove it from the mold or jar and store it in an airtight container in a cool, dry place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa creams contains curcumin, a key bioactive constituent responsible for its medicinal properties. Curcumin has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which help alleviate symptoms of altitude sickness. Turmerones, another active compound in Curcuma longa creams, has been found to have vasodilatory properties, widening blood vessels and improving oxygen delivery to the body's tissues. This can help counteract the oxygen deficiency often associated with altitude sickness. By reducing inflammation and improving circulation, Curcuma longa creams may help alleviate headaches, fatigue, and other symptoms commonly experienced at high elevations.
remedies.image_alt: Curcuma longa herbal creams for altitude sickness
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup coconut oil, 1/2 cup beeswax, 2 tablespoons turmeric powder (Curcuma longa), 2 tablespoons ginger powder, 2 tablespoons lavender oil, 2 tablespoons vitamin E oil.
Melt coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl in 30-second increments, stirring between each heating.
Once melted, stir in turmeric powder, ginger powder, and lavender oil until well combined.
Remove from heat and let cool for 5 minutes. Stir in vitamin E oil.
Pour mixture into a silicone candy mold or a small container. Let cool and harden completely before use. Store in a cool place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Panax ginseng creams contains bioactive constituents like ginsenosides and saponins, which have been found to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These properties can help reduce swelling and damage to the brain and body caused by altitude sickness. The ginsenosides, specifically, have been shown to improve blood flow and oxygen delivery to the brain and other tissues, which can help alleviate symptoms of altitude sickness such as headaches and fatigue. Additionally, saponins have been found to have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation and damage to the body caused by high altitude. By improving blood flow and reducing inflammation, Panax ginseng creams may help alleviate symptoms of altitude sickness and allow the body to adapt to high altitudes more quickly.
remedies.image_alt: Panax ginseng herbal creams for altitude sickness
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of distilled water, 1 tablespoon of Panax ginseng extract, 1/2 cup of shea butter, 1/4 cup of coconut oil, and 1 teaspoon of vitamin E oil.
In a double boiler, heat the distilled water over low heat. Add the Panax ginseng extract and stir until dissolved.
In a separate bowl, mix the shea butter, coconut oil, and vitamin E oil until smooth.
Slowly pour the ginseng-infused water into the shea butter mixture, stirring constantly.
Transfer the mixture to a container and refrigerate until solidified. Once set, apply the Panax ginseng cream as needed to alleviate altitude sickness symptoms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Valeriana officinalis creams contains valerenic acid and valeranone, which are active constituents that help with altitude sickness. These compounds have sedative and anti-inflammatory properties, which can reduce the severity of symptoms such as headaches and dizziness. The valerenic acid in Valeriana officinalis creams has a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help alleviate stress and anxiety caused by high altitudes. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory properties of valeranone can help reduce swelling and pain in the body. By reducing stress and inflammation, Valeriana officinalis creams may provide relief from altitude sickness symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Valeriana officinalis herbal creams for altitude sickness
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Valeriana officinalis roots, 1/2 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil, and 2 tablespoons of lavender essential oil.
Grind the dried Valeriana officinalis roots into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Add the ground Valeriana officinalis powder, vitamin E oil, and lavender essential oil to the melted coconut oil and beeswax mixture. Stir well to combine.
Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool until it reaches a thick, creamy consistency. Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool completely before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Schisandra chinensis creams contains a combination of active constituents like schisandrins, lignans, and polysaccharides. These compounds work together to improve circulation and increase oxygen delivery to the body's cells. Schisandrins, in particular, have been shown to enhance mitochondrial function, which helps the body adapt to high-altitude conditions. The lignans in Schisandra chinensis creams also exhibit antioxidant properties, protecting the body from oxidative stress caused by altitude sickness. By promoting healthy circulation and reducing oxidative damage, Schisandra chinensis creams may help alleviate symptoms associated with altitude sickness.
remedies.image_alt: Schisandra chinensis herbal creams for altitude sickness
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Schisandra chinensis berries, 1 cup of coconut oil, and 1/2 cup of beeswax.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl over a pot of simmering water.
Add the Schisandra chinensis berries to the melted mixture and let it steep for 2-3 hours.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean container, discarding the solids.
Let the mixture cool and solidify before whipping it into a creamy texture with a hand mixer or whisk.

faq_prevent: Drinking herbal tea may help prevent altitude sickness, but its effectiveness is not proven. Ginger tea, for example, can ease nausea and headaches, which are common symptoms of altitude sickness. However, drinking herbal tea won't replace the need to ascend gradually and stay hydrated. It might provide some relief, but more research is needed to confirm its benefits.
faq_safe: While herbal teas can help alleviate altitude sickness symptoms, consuming them daily isn't a straightforward answer. Some teas, like ginkgo biloba and ginger, can help with oxygen flow and nausea. However, excessive consumption of certain herbs might interact with other medications or have adverse effects. Moderation is key when it comes to herbal teas.
faq_timeline: Herbal teas like ginger and ginkgo biloba may help alleviate altitude sickness symptoms. Their effects can be noticed within 30 minutes to 2 hours after consumption. As you ascend to higher elevations, these teas can help reduce dizziness and nausea, allowing you to adapt more comfortably.
faq_time: At high altitudes, the air is thinner and your body needs extra help to cope. Drinking herbal tea in the morning can help. Ginger tea, in particular, is good at this time as it can help ease nausea and boost energy.
faq_interaction: Herbal teas can interact with prescription medications for altitude sickness. For example, if you're taking medication like acetazolamide to help your body adjust to high altitude, drinking certain herbal teas like ginkgo biloba or echinacea might affect how well the medication works. This could make altitude sickness symptoms worse.
faq_pregnant: When you're pregnant and at high altitude, herbal teas can be tempting to ease symptoms. However, some herbal teas might not be safe during pregnancy. Ginseng and yarrow teas, for example, can cause problems. It's best to stick with herbal teas that are known to be safe during pregnancy, like peppermint or ginger.
faq_breastfeeding: When breastfeeding at high altitudes, drinking herbal teas might be tempting. However, some herbal teas can pass into breast milk and affect baby's health. Peppermint, ginger, and licorice root teas are known to be safe in moderation. But it's best to avoid teas with unknown effects on babies, like chamomile or passionflower.
title_2: 10 Herbal Creams to Help Hikers Overcome Altitude Sickness
